subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Gravitational Microlensing
description: Learn Gravitational Microlensing in one minute.

hook.text: When a star with a planet passes in front of a background star, gravity bends the light and briefly magnifies it.

buildup.text: The planet creates an extra spike in the magnification curve. This method can find planets thousands of light-years away.

discovery.text: OGLE-2005-BLG-390Lb was found this way—a cold, distant world 21,000 light-years away, one of the farthest known.

twist.text: Microlensing events are one-time—they never repeat. You get one chance to observe each detection.

climax.text: NASA's Roman Space Telescope will use microlensing to detect thousands of exoplanets, including free-floating ones.

punchline.text: Gravity bends light. One brief flash reveals a hidden world. No second chances.

quiz.question: What is unique about gravitational microlensing detections?
options: They are one-time events that never repeat
They can only detect gas giant planets
They require the planet to transit its star
